message:
fix(vectordb,sandbox,sdk): clippy lint cleanups, safe LEB128 cast, and escape_control_chars bug fix

Three targeted code quality improvements across different crates:

vectordb integration tests:
- Suppress clippy::needless_pass_by_value on TestLog methods that
  intentionally take T by value for ergonomic test assertions
- Replace eager .ok_or(format!(...)) with lazy .ok_or_else(|| format!(...))
  to avoid unnecessary string allocations on the happy path (4 sites)
- Rename ambiguous variable token_uw → token_upsert for clarity
- Remove redundant .clone() on credential_id field init shorthand
- Use method reference Value::is_array instead of closure

fcp-sandbox wasi.rs (LEB128 decoder):
- Replace unsafe `as u32` cast on shift value with
  u32::try_from(shift).ok()? to prevent silent truncation on
  pathological inputs — addresses clippy::cast_possible_truncation

fcp-sdk formatting.rs (escape_control_chars):
- Fix bug where escape_control_chars was escaping newlines, carriage
  returns, and tabs, destroying whitespace structure in multi-line text.
  Now preserves \n, \r, and \t while still escaping truly problematic
  control characters like \x00 (null) and \x1b (escape)
- Add regression test verifying whitespace preservation and control
  character escaping behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
